title: Entity 83
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 83: "'''Cone Spiders'''"}}
==Description== Cone spiders are a clade of arthropods that can be found hunting throughout a wide variety of levels within the backrooms. Cone spiders have a large cone, resembling a man-made traffic cone, protecting their upper body, while their mandibles, 10 legs, and eyes are exposed. It is currently unknown how cone spiders receive their shell, as no juvenile specimens of the species have been discovered, and chemical analysis of these shells simply show polyvinyl chloride, the same material traffic cones on earth are made out of. Most species of cone spiders prefer dark and moist levels that at least have a 'natural' semblance to it such as looking like a forest. While forests are the general habitat of the cone spiders, due to their immense genetic diversity, many species prefer completely different habitats, Theories exist that cone spiders have a home level due to the fact that no permanent and sustained population of cone spiders has been found, although no way to enter such a level has been discovered as of yet. ==Origin== The name "cone spider" is a misnomer, as recent genetic studies indicate that cone spiders share much of their DNA with arthropods of the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]], particularly decapods. ==Species== Documented below are an accessible list of known species of Cone spiders.
  • Common Cone Spider
  • Frigid Cone Spider
  • Luminous Pygmy Cone Spider
  • Giant Cone Spider

{{Entity Class|no=83|Dan=2|Hab=Forest or Plains Levels, Hypothesized Home Level|Int=1|Agg=1}} ==Species Description== Common cone spiders are the most common species of cone spider, found in a wide plethora of levels of the [[Level List|Backrooms]]. They are around one-half to two-thirds of the average wanderer's size and typically thrive in natural forest-like levels or plains-like levels, but 'natural formations' can suffice to them. They can also be found migrating through other levels, stopping by for food. The common cone spiders are genetically stable, giving them a long lifespan, where their cone-like body can accumulate in damages that can recover and form imperfections. Their cones are orange colored, whilst their bodies are black. ==Species Behavior== Common cone spiders wander the level, burrowing to create traps. They tend to attack smaller entities, using their mandibles to create deep gashes, while using their legs to immobilize the prey. They tend to attack humans when threatened and occasionally when starving, but serious injuries are rather unlikely. They can be scared away easily, simply stand up straight and look as intimidating as possible. ==Species Biology== The average common cone spider can be identified by an orange traffic cone, usually out of place. Long-term experiments predict that common cone spiders have such extremely efficient metabolism, such to the point that they typically won't starve of food or water for years, although this hypothesis has never been tested. Common cone spiders are awake at all times, but rest to gain more strength.

{{Entity Class|no=83|Dan=4|Hab=Wintry Levels, Hypothesized Home Level|Int=3|Agg=2}} ==Species Description== This species of cone spider prefers to burrow in snow and ice instead of the typical materials that most species burrow into. Frigid cone spiders seem to attack wanderers more frequently than any other species, but are at the same time slow creatures, most likely trying to conserve energy while dealing with the cold. ==Species Behavior== Frigid cone spiders are the most aggressive species of cone spider currently known. They can usually be found burrowing in snow or ice. They are the only known cone spider species that will actively hunt humans ==Species Biology== Frigid cone spiders are much larger than their common counterparts and can be identified by a blue traffic cone, usually out of place. Frigid cone spiders usually range around the size of the average adult human. They have longer and stronger arms used to dig into ice, and their mandibles are much more powerful to deal with larger prey.

{{Entity Class|no=83|Dan=0|Hab=Levels with Electricity or Metal, Hypothesized Home Level|Int=1|Agg=1}} ==Species Description== Luminous pygmy cone spiders are a rare species of cone spider that typically live indoors in packs. They are much smaller than the common cone spider, being on average around the size of a coconut crab. Their cones exist in two states: a dark and a bright state. During the dark state, the cones are partially translucent and appear to be hollow in nature. In the bright state, they seem to glow a specific color brightly, illuminating their surroundings. It seems that these luminous pygmy cone spiders are capable of controlling their cone lights at will, but it is not known why they have this trait or when they do it. All that is known that in packs they do it synchronously. ==Species Behavior== Luminous pygmy cone-spiders tend to mind their own business and move in packs, climbing the walls of the level they reside in and resting. What is known is that they seem to be siphon electricity from wherever they can and also consume various rusts and oxides that can be found on metals within levels. ==Species Biology== Luminous pygmy cone-spiders appear to feed off these oxides and refine them internally into nano electronics located within their partially translucent cones, using the electricity they consume to power these nano-scale lights at will. Without these lights, their cones appear to be a desaturated orange and white striped pattern, with their main bodies also having a desaturated orange color.

{{Entity Class|no=83|Dan=4|Hab=Marine Levels, Hypothesized Home Level|Int=2|Agg=1}} ==Species Description== Giant cone spiders have a physical appearance reminiscent to that of the common cone spider, although they are also similar in appearance to squids of the [[The Fontrooms|Frontrooms]]. Their size is twice that of a manatee, which is already larger than a human. ==Species Behavior== Giant cone spiders are unable to attack anything due to their extremely low metabolism and therefore extremely slow speeds and mind their business. They are generally solitary but in large oceanic levels have been seen in massive packs filter-feeding the waters. Perhaps their slow speeds were what was responsible for their current conservation status of '''Critical''', approaching extinction. ==Species Biology== Giant cone spiders are massive, and so their metabolism is vastly decreased. They are gentle filter-feeders, consuming particles found in the waters that they live in. They are amphibious but heavily prefer living on water. Giant cone spiders also have extreme resilience against harsh conditions, almost like tardigrades. They are capable of surviving boiling water and freezing water. The cones of giant cone spiders are made of a metallic material, compared to the softer materials that comprise the cones of the common cone spiders. Most of their cones are typically covered in rocks and marine life.
